title: Real-Time Analytics Dashboard
description: High-performance dashboard for monitoring system metrics with live WebSocket updates and interactive charts.
longDescription: A production-grade analytics platform built to handle millions of events per day. The dashboard provides real-time visibility into system health, user behavior, and business KPIs with sub-second latency.
problem: Ops and product teams needed a single source of truth for system and business metrics with minimal latency, without overloading existing backends.
solution: A dedicated real-time pipeline with WebSocket ingestion, Redis-backed session state, and edge-deployed API routes so the UI stays responsive under load.
architecture: Server-sent events for real-time streams, edge-deployed API routes for low latency, Redis for session and cache layer, PostgreSQL with read replicas for analytics queries.
challenges: Managing connection scaling for 10k+ concurrent WebSocket clients; optimizing chart re-renders for 60fps; designing a query layer that stays responsive under load.
results: 50% reduction in time-to-insight for ops team; 99.9% uptime over 12 months; sub-200ms p95 for dashboard load.

title: Component Design System
description: Accessible, themable design system with 50+ components and full documentation site.
longDescription: An internal design system that powers 15+ product teams. Built with accessibility as a first-class concern and full dark mode, RTL, and keyboard navigation support.
architecture: Monorepo with packages for tokens, components, and docs. CSS variables for theming; compound components for flexibility; comprehensive Storybook with a11y addon.
challenges: Keeping bundle size minimal while supporting tree-shaking; maintaining consistent API across 50+ components; documenting usage and accessibility for each component.
results: 40% faster feature development for consuming teams; zero critical a11y issues in audits; single source of truth for brand and UX.

title: Mobile-First Payment Flow
description: Streamlined checkout experience for mobile with saved methods and one-tap pay.
longDescription: Redesigned the payment experience for a fintech product, focusing on conversion and trust. Supports cards, wallets, and bank redirects with a unified, accessible flow.
architecture: React Native with shared TypeScript types and GraphQL client. Backend: Node.js API, Stripe for payment processing, idempotency keys for safe retries.
challenges: Handling 3DS and bank redirects in-app without losing context; ensuring PCI-friendly implementation (no card data on our servers); A/B testing without fragmenting the codebase.
results: 23% increase in checkout completion on mobile; 60% of users adopt one-tap pay within 30 days; 0 payment disputes attributed to UX.

title: Internal API Gateway
description: Central gateway for service-to-service and external API traffic with auth, rate limits, and observability.
longDescription: A custom API gateway that replaced a third-party solution, giving full control over routing, authentication, and telemetry. Handles 100M+ requests per day.
architecture: Go services behind Kubernetes Ingress; JWT and OAuth2 validation at the edge; distributed rate limiting with Redis; OpenTelemetry for traces and metrics.
challenges: Minimizing latency added by auth and logging; designing rate limits that work across regions; safe rollout of routing changes.
results: 30% lower p99 latency vs previous gateway; single pane of glass for API observability; zero-downtime config and cert rotations.

title: Developer Docs Platform
description: Searchable, versioned documentation with code samples and interactive API reference.
longDescription: A docs site that pulls from OpenAPI specs and Markdown, with full-text search, version switching, and runnable code samples in the browser.
architecture: Next.js static generation with MDX; Algolia for search; OpenAPI codegen for API reference; sandboxed iframe for code execution.
challenges: Keeping generated API docs in sync with 20+ services; search relevance across versions; safe execution of user-provided code samples.
results: 50% reduction in support tickets about API usage; docs consistently in top 3 search results for product terms.
